channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: President Trump to host Israeli PM at the White House
item.description: Among the most important topics between the two will be how to keep the ceasefire deal with Hamas intact.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 'rare' price for US military aid to Ukraine called 'fair' by Zelenskyy
item.description: President Donald Trump suggested Ukraine begin offering critical minerals to the U.S. in exchange for military aid, and President Volodymyr Zelenskyy seemingly welcomed the idea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio governor orders government employees to return to office, following Trump's lead
item.description: Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine signed an executive order Tuesday directing state employees to return to office by Monday, March 17, following President Donald Trump's lead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former President Joe Biden signs with powerful Hollywood agency
item.description: The 46th president has signed with Creative Artists Agency to handle his professional life after leaving office, the agency announced.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of illegals arrested in Trump’s home county in Florida
item.description: Amid President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown, U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officers arrested 32 criminal illegal immigrants in the president’s Florida home county.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate panel advances Tulsi Gabbard nomination for DNI
item.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee on Tuesday advanced Tulsi Gabbard’s nomination to become the next director of national intelligence (DNI), putting her in position to get confirmed by the full chamber after key senators threw their support behind her. The 9-8 vote was along party lines.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard advances out of Intel Committee in boost to confirmation odds
item.description: The key committee vote on Tulsi Gabbard for Director of National Intelligence (DNI) determined whether her nomination would move to the floor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US begins flying migrants to Guantanamo Bay
item.description: The Trump administration has begun flying detained illegal migrants from the U.S. to Guantanamo Bay in Cuba, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk tightens grip on federal government as Democrats raise alarms
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk is rapidly consolidating control over large swaths of the federal government with President Donald Trump ’s blessing, sidelining career officials, gaining access to sensitive databases and dismantling a leading source of humanitarian assistance.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says El Salvador offers to accept deportees from US of any nationality, including Americans
item.description: SAN SALVADOR, El Salvador (AP) — U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio left El Salvador on Tuesday with an agreement from that country's president to accept deportees from the U.S. of any nationality, including violent American criminals now imprisoned in the United States.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Pam Bondi AG nomination
item.description: The Senate on Monday teed up a final vote on Pam Bondi's nomination to lead the Justice Department. The chamber voted 52-46 to limit debate. Absent an agreement with Democrats, that puts a final confirmation vote on the docket for the early hours of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: Local congressman reacts to inspectors general dismissed by President Trump
item.description: In the flurry of Trump's executive orders covering immigration raids, federal firings, funding freezes and tariff negotiations, the dismissing of 17 inspector generals has gotten little notice, but according to San Diego Congressman Mike Levin, this is a bigger issue than most understand.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, Fox 5

item.title: White House defends its 'Fork in the Road' offer to federal employees
item.description: The White House says the federal employees union is doing its members a disservice by urging them not to resign with the promise of administrative leave until September.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Association representing thousands of FBI agents appeals to Congress to protect their jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The association representing thousands of FBI agents urged congressional leaders Monday to protect the jobs of employees at risk of punishment or possibly termination over their participation in investigations into the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Judge in nation's capital extends block on Trump administration federal funding freeze
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A judge in the nation's capital kept a temporary block on a Trump administration plan for a freeze on federal funding Monday after some nonprofit groups said they're still struggling to get promised grants and loans. U.S. District Judge Loren L.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrat places blanket hold on Trump’s State Department picks over targeting of USAID
item.description: Sen. Brian Schatz (D-Hawaii), a member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, announced Monday he will place a blanket hold on all of President Trump’s nominees to the State Department until the president backs off his effort to shut down USAID, the nation’s chief foreign assistance agency.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury Secretary Bessent, tapped to run CFPB, orders staff to halt work
item.description: Staff at the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau were instructed by email to cease much of their work. Bessent, a wealthy Wall Street investor, replaces Rohit Chopra, who was fired on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Wamsley, MPR News

item.title: President Donald Trump agrees to pause tariffs on Mexico
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum said Monday that after a conversation with U.S. President Donald Trump that the planned tariffs are on hold for a month, a statement confirmed by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump kept his tariffs pledge. Is he ready for the fallout? Are Americans? What to know
item.description: The Budget Lab at Yale University estimates Trump’s tariffs would cost the average American household $1,000 to $1,200 in annual purchasing power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trial starts for alleged ringleader of massive COVID food fraud
item.description: A federal jury was selected Monday in the second criminal trial to stem from a lengthy federal investigation into Feeding Our Future. The judge put security measures in place after an alleged attempted bribe of jurors in the first trial in the case.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis, Florida GOP tensions spill out into open
item.description: Tensions are spilling out into the open between Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) and members of his own party in the Sunshine State, pointing to a new discordant era of GOP-led politics in a state that has trended red over the last few cycles.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk's X sues Lego, Nestlé and more brands, accusing them of advertising boycott
item.description: The new defendants added into the legal filing included Lego, Nestlé, Tyson Foods, Abbott Laboratories, Colgate-Palmolive, Pinterest and Shell International.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim and Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump imposes tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China, raising prospect of higher costs for U.S. consumers
item.description: President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an order to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada and China — fulfilling one of his post-campaign commitments to voters that also carries the risk of sparking higher inflation and disrupting businesses across North America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis threatens Veto amid heated showdown
item.description: State lawmakers voted to pass the TRUMP act, which aims to support the president on immigration enforcement. Now, all eyes are on Governor DeSantis who promises a veto.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Political parties explain Hillsborough County's conservative flip
item.description: As of Thursday, Hillsborough County records showed the number of active registered Republicans was bigger than the number of registered Democrats. The difference as of Friday was 307 voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How non-violent undocumented immigrants could be swept up in raids
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – In two weeks of the second Trump administration, the White House is staying true to tough immigration policies with ICE raids across the country. Several have happened in the tri-state area.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge temporarily blocks Trump admin's freeze on federal grants, loans
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a Trump administration freeze on federal grants and loans that could total trillions of dollars.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
